# Test fixtures — real room snapshots

These fixtures are **real terrain + room objects** pulled from the official
Screeps API (`screeps.com`) and committed so deterministic smoke tests can run
against the actual problem room without network access.

They are generated by [`Bot/scripts/fetch-room-fixture.mjs`](../../scripts/fetch-room-fixture.mjs):

```sh
# from Bot/
node scripts/fetch-room-fixture.mjs W52S13 shard3
```

The script reads the API token from `SCREEPS_TOKEN` (env) or
`<repo>/Strategist/.env`. The token is never printed or written into a fixture.
Do not hand-edit the fixtures; re-run the script to refresh them.

## `<room>.terrain.json`

```jsonc
{
  "room": "W52S13",
  "shard": "shard3",
  "encoded": "<2500-char digit string>", // index = y*50 + x
  "grid": [["plain" | "wall" | "swamp", ...x50], ...x50] // grid[y][x]
}
```

Terrain digit → tile mapping (mirrors `API/src/modules/rooms.ts#digitToTile`,
the same mapping the bot's `distanceTransform`/planner expect):

| digit | tile  |
| ----- | ----- |
| `0`   | plain |
| `1`   | wall  |
| `2`   | swamp |
| `3`   | wall  |

> Note: the terrain tile **under** a source / controller / mineral is encoded as
> `wall` by Screeps (the object sits on a wall-masked tile). This is expected and
> matches in-game `Room.Terrain`. The planner uses the *objects* fixture for those
> fixed positions, not the terrain mask.

## `<room>.objects.json`

Normalized, stable subset of the `game/room-objects` response (transient objects
— creeps, dropped resources, tombstones, ruins, construction sites, nukes — are
excluded):

```jsonc
{
  "room": "W52S13",
  "shard": "shard3",
  "sources": [{ "x": 18, "y": 3 }, ...],
  "controller": { "x": 5, "y": 37 } /* | null */,
  "mineral": { "x": 21, "y": 47, "mineralType": "H" } /* | null */,
  "structures": [{ "type": "extension", "x": 12, "y": 25 }, ...], // includes spawns
  "spawns": [{ "x": 13, "y": 26 }, ...]
}
```

`structures` includes the spawn(s) (also surfaced separately under `spawns`).
Lists are sorted (sources/structures by position) so the fixture is diff-stable
across re-fetches.
